programme.desc: PRS and Psychic/Medium Chip Coffey investigate the famed Hotel Conneaut in northern Pennsylvania. Heavy doors opening on their own, sightings of a boy, a bride and a menacing butcher have the hotel's staff on edge.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Haunted Sex Dungeon
programme.desc: Psychic/Medium Chip Coffey and Chad Calek join PRS for this unusual case of a sprawling mansion on Greyhaven Island near Detroit. Ken, the owner, hears voices and banging and it has him feeling both watched and cursed; a prisoner in his own home.

programme.title: Paranormal State: The Sickness
programme.desc: Something is making Lisa and her family sick. Is it natural or paranormal? Ryan and the team uncover the tragic history surrounding Lisa's home--with shocking connections to respiratory illness.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Good Vs. Evil
programme.desc: PRS visits a western Pennsylvania couple wracked by a torrent of paranormal activity.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Get Out of This House
programme.desc: When an aggressive spirit stalks a young family, PRS is called in to investigate. When it is determined that the source of the haunting may have been unknowingly invited in, Ryan races to identify and hopefully defeat the entity before it's too late.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Armley Mills
programme.desc: Once the centre of industry Armley Mills is now a museum that is said to house the phantoms of many a lost soul. From children who were caught in the machinery to a bad tempered floorwalker who doesn’t like people in his mill.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Carr House
programme.desc: Part of a once splendorous manor and a history that goes back as far as Robin Hood, Carr House is the sight of many a paranormal occurrence.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Hill House_PT1
programme.desc: What was once a private home before becoming a sanctuary for the elderly, the abandoned Hill House is now an eerie shadow of its former glory, that houses empty rooms, strange noises and a plethora of paranormal activity.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Hill House_PT2
programme.desc: Part one saw the team seemingly getting stalked by something, and in this part it shows its hand.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Village Church Farm
programme.desc: A museum like no other, this small collection of antiquated buildings is said to be the home of several spirits. The team get more than they bargained for as objects move; strange noises are caught on camera and the best EVP response to date.

programme.desc: Rico wants to move to England to care for his ailing mother, but his intense online relationship with Ja'mari, an international male model, is giving him second thoughts about leaving.

programme.desc: Barney poses as a prospective rock-music recording artist to get evidence linking an organized crime figurehead played by guest star William Windom to the sudden death of a beautiful recording star who was being blackmailed by the syndicate.

programme.desc: Casey poses as a young peasant girl from Europe imported to be the bride of Joe Corvin, the top man at getting underworld millions into Swiss banks.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Sink or Swim
programme.desc: 1905 Hand-crank Thomas Edison gramophone with cylindrical records; a discontinued Addictor 190 Mini Boat; a rare 1897 Regina music box.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Carnies & Armories
programme.desc: A tough group of bidders team up to take down Ton and Allen at auction. After a couple of wins, the guys try to sell an arsenal used by Native Americans and some vintage carnival gear. But will they get conned?

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Mr. Haff Goes to Washington
programme.desc: Ton and Allen invade the Nation's capital, but meet strong resistance from local bidders. The guys uncover a Civil War era black powder revolver and a stash of South African gold coins!

programme.title: Auction Hunters: It's Raining Ton
programme.desc: The guys take Big Sis to her first auction - and when things get ugly, she becomes their secret weapon. Ton tests out a "blob" and Allen gets shot with a riot gun.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Money Makin' Monster Machines
programme.desc: The Auction Hunters team goes on the attack & ends up on a collision course with a hardened veteran auction buyer. They uncover a collection of speedy off-road vehicles and some hardcore heavy metal from the turn-of the-century!

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Auction Hunters, Ink.
programme.desc: Autographed 1960s Bob Shaw Swing Gate tattoo machine; Terry Ives competition 22hp chainsaw; six foot chainsaw-carved cedar bear.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: The Big Score

programme.title: Auction Hunters: The Chi-Town Showdown
programme.desc: Metal Victorian-era burial casket; 1870s glass fire extinguisher grenades; a mechanical 1896 handcrafted Chicago fire station alarm box.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Blazing Saddles

programme.title: Teen Mom: How Many Chances?

programme.title: Teen Mom: Fallout

programme.title: Teen Mom: Moving On
programme.desc: Amber and Gary's arguing finally reaches a boiling point, while Catelynn struggles to find a home when her mother moves away. Newly-single mum Maci has a change of heart.

programme.title: The Maury Show: My Daughter Told Me You Cheated!
programme.desc: Tammy suspects her husband Johnnie is sleeping with Renee and is Robert listening to his sister and cheating on his teen fiancée Raven? Plus, did Tom sleep with his best friend’s wife Angela? Marriages are on cliff’s edge today!

programme.title: The Maury Show: This Test Will Tell…Do I really Have 10 Kids?
programme.desc: Did Tiffany’s fiancé Deon get her lesbian best friend pregnant? Then, William believes that his 2 of his 6 kids with ex-wife Nicole aren’t his and could be the father of another’s woman’s kid! Our DNA tests reveal the truth!

programme.title: The Maury Show: Did My Fiancé Cheat with Over 50 Women and My Best Friend?
programme.desc: Did Tiffany’s fiancé get her lesbian best friend pregnant? Then, Don and Amy are back for another lie detector test! This time Don wants to know if Amy is sleeping with his brother! Will they ace their tests?

programme.title: The Maury Show: 5 Men Tested… Are Any of Them My Baby's Dad?
programme.desc: Teshinya suspects that Clarence is picking up women at church and went undercover to get proof! Desirae is 17 and 5 months pregnant, but thinks her boyfriend Chris is cheating with her friend!

programme.title: The Maury Show: Did My Wife Betray Me By Sleeping with My Son?
programme.desc: Romel, his wife Chanel and her sister Nakiya are back because he thinks Chanel is sleeping with his 21-year-old son Romel Jr. who’s dating Nakiya! Later, Reggie cheated on Teri 9 times, but does he actually have a sex addiction?

programme.title: The Maury Show: Maury Guest Updates…I'm Back with a Secret!

programme.title: The Maury Show: Is My Sister's Boyfriend My Baby's Dad?

programme.desc: Top 10 Secrets and Mysteries looks at places that are said to have healing power including Lourdes where people have been cured by drinking water from a cave, Machu Picchu, Stonehenge, the Dead Sea, the Ganges.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: MYSTERIOUS TEXTS
programme.desc: Top 10 Secrets and Mysteries looks at mysteries related to ancient texts including the Egyptian Book of the Dead, the Torah, the Secrets of Fulcanelli, the Tao Te Ching, the Vedas.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: MYSTERIES OF ANCIENT CULTURES
programme.desc: Top 10 Secrets and Mysteries looks at mysteries associated with ancient cultures including Lemuria, the Indus Valley civilization of Harappa, the Forgotten Kingdoms of Tibet, the Olmec civilization of Central America.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: SECRET CULTS AND ORDERS
programme.desc: Top 10 Secrets and Mysteries looks at secret cults and mysterious orders including Skull and Bones at Yale University, the Freemasons, Thule and Vril Societies, the Bullingdon Club, the Hermetic Order of the Golden Dawn

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1975, 15-year-old Martha Moxley is murdered in Belle Haven, Connecticut. The case goes cold until 2002, when Michael Skakel, a family member, is convicted based on new evidence. Skakel maintains his innocence.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: On October 11, 1998, Dr. Harold Shipman is tried for murdering fifteen elderly patients. Exhumation and evidence of lethal morphine lead to his conviction. He is later found to have killed 215 people.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In January 2002, Ashley Pond and Miranda Gaddis disappear in Portland. Neighbor Ward Weaver, a suspect with a violent history, is later found guilty of their murders and sentenced to life in prison.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: A picture-perfect Canadian couple Karla Homolka and Paul Bernardo join to perform torture and mutilation on young victims - crimes that are virtually unspeakable. Two teenage girls were raped and murdered, and the couple even filmed themselves doing it.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In July 2000, Sarah Payne is abducted and murdered. Roy Whiting, a convicted sex offender, is found guilty and sentenced to life in 2001. Sarah’s case leads to the implementation of “Sarah’s Law” in the UK in 2011.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: On June 27, 2005, Dennis Rader, known as the BTK killer, pleads guilty to ten murders he committed in Wichita, Kansas, from 1974 to 1994. After a journalist's article reignites the case, Rader is arrested and sentenced to ten life terms without parole.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In September 1987, Simon Dale is murdered. His ex-wife, Susan Wilberforce, is acquitted of the crime but later convicted of fraud and sentenced to seven years. The murder remains unsolved.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: Nathaniel Bar-Jonah, a paedophile, is convicted in 2002 for multiple crimes but not for the 1996 disappearance of Zachary Ramsay. Zachary’s body is never found, and Bar-Jonah dies in prison in 2008.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In January 2002, fashion journalist Christa Worthington is found murdered in her Truro home. Christopher McCowen is convicted in 2006 of her murder, robbery, and rape, despite his claims of innocence and other DNA found at the scene.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: VOLCANOES
programme.desc: Volcanoes are Earth’s geologic architects. They’ve created more than 80 percent of our planet’s surface and their explosive force has crafted both mountains and craters.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Java: Land of Fire
programme.desc: In the Pacific Ring of Fire island of Java, George explores new ash eruptions from Anak Krakatau, whose 1883 eruption shook the world. He travels to Merapi and descends into Kawah Ijen to explore the world's largest lake of sulfuric acid.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Antarctica
programme.desc: From "Fin Del Mundo," George sails on a Russian icebreaker to Antarctica. After braving stormy Drake passage, he explores volcanic Deception Island, camps in Antarctic snow and paddles a kayak through icebergs calved off melting glaciers.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Winds Of Autumn
programme.desc: This year he starts off by getting prepared in a wind tunnel, then he and his chase partner Mark Robinson head to Newfoundland for more wild weather action. Then, it's off to another encounter where the wild wind fans a raging inferno.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Hurricane Triple Threat
programme.desc: George chases deadly hurricanes in the USA: Gustav in Louisiana and Hanna in North Carolina. He then flies with USAF Hurricane Hunters into Ike over Cuba, meeting it again as it makes a wild destructive landfall in Galveston, Texas.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Wild Water
programme.desc: In this episode, George investigates the wild side of water in dangerous class 5 river rapids, a flooded cave, and even in the ocean surrounded by massive Great White Sharks.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Thunder Down Under
programme.desc: In Australia George teams up with storm chaser Jimmy Deguara to witness summer storms in the outback. While in Oz, he dives the magnificent Great Barrier Reef, and explores just how painful is the sting of the venomous Box Jellyfish.

programme.title: Angry Planet: West Coast Winter Weather
programme.desc: On Canada's rugged West Coast, George ice-climbs a frozen waterfall, uses ski-skins to climb Mt.Garibaldi to an alpine lake, visits a storm-watching lodge on British Columbia's Long Beach, home of winter surfing and massive Pacific storms.

programme.title: Naked Science: Making Mountains
programme.desc: The Rockies. Magnificent and awe-inspiring mysterious, the Rockies are one of the longest mountain ranges in the world. Naked Science investigates the geological processes that build these massive mountains.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Shore To Shore
programme.desc: Our seafaring nature has translated well to the space age, seeking to sail out into the unknown in search of riches of one kind or another.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Mars: Adapt Or Leave
programme.desc: As the latest successes and failures come and go Mars continues to give up her secrets.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Emissaries
programme.desc: Our solar system is vast, from our own star the Sun to Earth is nearly 150 million kilometers or 1 Astronomical Unit.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Danger, Astreroids Ahead

programme.title: The New Frontier: Stars Above
programme.desc: Stars are a bit like human beings. They can be warm or cold, they come in all kinds of shapes and sizes.

programme.title: The New Frontier: The Dark Side
programme.desc: Perhaps that American politician was right. There are indeed things we know we don’t know.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Atmosphere
programme.desc: How do astronauts cope with living in space?

programme.title: Jungle Planet

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Mister Mom

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Scared Straight
programme.desc: Dog tracks a fugitive who is on the run from the law for the first time. Reading the file Dog knows the young man's life is at the crossroads. Will he be able to track down this wanna-be-gangsta in time to convince him to make the right choice?

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: All My Children

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Bounty Baby

programme.title: Strange Evidence

programme.title: Warbirds of WWII

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: 1942 and Hitler has the totally barking idea of using educated dogs to do a soldier's job. The Nazi leader wants to create fearsome canines to help him win the Second World War

programme.title: History Of Weapons: Attack From Above

programme.title: Greatest Escapes of WWII
programme.desc: The German mass breakout that stunned wartime Britain, the astonishing secret behind the daring escape of an American bomber crew trapped in the Soviet Union, and the Trojan Horse of Stalag Luft Three.

programme.title: Hitler's Secret Sex Life: Body Image

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: Near the Czech border, a secret German site, "The Giant," tests The Bell, powered by Serum 525, turning all who touch it into rotting living zombies.

programme.desc: It's a hot day in Elwood City, and a total blackout leaves its residents without electricity! Will Arthur and DW survive without AC and TV? Binky's baby sister Mei-Lin isn't keen on learning to walk and would rather stick to crawling.

programme.title: Arthur: Arthur Babysits/Arthur's Cousin Catastrophe
programme.desc: You think running a marathon is hard? Try babysitting for the Terrible Tibble Twins. Next, Arthur's family is hosting the once-a-year-is-more-than-enough family reunion. But a major thunderstorm turns this into a reunion to remember.

programme.title: Arthur: The Butler Did...What?/The Trouble with Trophies
programme.desc: Bailey is missing! Muffy discovers a series of clues and is left wondering, who exactly IS Bailey? Then, everyone loves getting trophies, right? It turns out that Fern could not care less about them.

programme.title: Arthur: Arthur's Numbers Nightmare/Brain Gets Hooked
programme.desc: When the gang discovers a list of their class rankings in Principal Haney's office, they are all taken for a surprise. Brain gets hooked on the latest tween reality show and then starts to ignore his friends and his schoolwork.

programme.title: Arthur: What Is That Thing?/Buster's Best Behavior
programme.desc: Arthur and friends are all faced with seemingly impossible tasks, from fixing a model biplane to babysitting D.W. And, Buster knows he's fun, but he's beginning to feel like he's not particularly good at anything.

programme.title: Arthur: Arthur and The Crunch Cereal Contest/D.W. Flips
programme.desc: A year's supply of Crunch Cereal is in store for the person who can write the new Crunch Cereal jingle, and Arthur is sure he can do it. Next, how hard can it be to do a cartwheel on a balance beam? D.W. is about to find out.

programme.title: Arthur: Flippity Francine/Muffy Takes the Wheel
programme.desc: When Muffy posts a goofy video of Francine on the Muffington Post, Francine worries she will become the laughing stock of the entire school. And it's the 1st Annual Lakewood Derby and Muffy's revved up!

programme.title: Arthur: Francine's Split Decision/Muffy Goes Metropolitan
programme.desc: Lakewood Elementary is sure to win the big bowling tournament on Saturday, but Francine's cousin is having his Bar Mitzvah at the same time! Muffy happily accepts an invitation to spend the weekend in the bustling Crown City.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Barney and the kids take imaginary trips to different countries with the magical caboose.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Best In Show - Ducks And Fish
programme.desc: When the kids decide to have a Dog Show to show off their pets, BJ feels a little left out. He doesn't have a dog, and he sure wishes he did! A friend from the park shares her dog with BJ so he can join in the fun.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Grace’s food drive isn’t going very well until Melanie offers to help. Melanie comes up with the perfect idea to get people to donate – put on a movie premiere in the caboose!

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Riff can’t find his drumsticks anywhere. Barney helps Baby Bop explore many different jobs.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: BJ learns a lesson about bragging when, playing superhero CAPTAIN PICKLES, he tells everyone that he can leap over the moon!

programme.title: Barney and Friends: BJ's Really Cool House
programme.desc: Although the park may be a home away from home for Barney and his friends, BJ wants to build his own "totally awesome, completely cool" house. While BJ works on his house, Barney and the children learn all about houses and homes.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Baby Bop isn’t satisfied with the things she can do. When a wheelchair-bound child is unintentionally neglected by his friends, Barney steps in to help.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Caring Hearts
programme.desc: A special project provides the perfect opportunity for Barney, Baby Bop and BJ to learn about love and relationships the "heart" way as they make a "Friends and Family Tree."

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Coming on Strong
programme.desc: Exercise is good for everybody and every body! From running to touching toes to jumping jacks to some favorite songs and games, Barney and his friends grow strong while having fun with exercise.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Artless
programme.desc: Two snobbish art-collecting aliens arrive in Florence and begin stealing great works of art.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Ring of Fire
programme.desc: Krang sends Shredder to set up a special lens that will focus the sun's rays to create a devastating heat ray.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Irish Jig Is Up
programme.desc: Krang and Shredder decide to use Ireland as their base for the Technodrome. With a device called the Rainbow Trans-Charmer, the villains intend to turn harmless little creatures like sheep and rabbits into slavering beasts.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Shredder's New Sword
programme.desc: In Dimension X Krang is using a long-range scanner and discovers a rare metal buried in a ruined castle in England. The metal turns out to be King Arthur's legendary sword, Excalibur.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Lost Queen of Atlantis
programme.desc: Shredder, Rocksteady and Bebop are searching for a valuable energy source in Athens and discover that it is April's new necklace.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Turtles on the Orient Express
programme.desc: April is covering the revival of the Orient Express. Shredder is on the train with Rocksteady and Bebop carrying the supercharger, a device to accelerate the train to super speed.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): April Gets in Dutch
programme.desc: April is covering the showing of the world-famed Duchess Diamond. Krang wants the diamond, a flawless stone, to focus the rays on his laser dimension blade.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Northern Lights Out
programme.desc: Eric Red seizes both April and Professor Swenson's map to the underground volcanoes in Scandinavia to tap into the molten energy and melt the polar ice caps.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Elementary, My Dear Turtle
programme.desc: The Turtles are in England, when they are taken back to the past, via an experimental atomic clock and must team up with Sherlock Holmes and Doctor Watson in order to get the clock back and return to the present.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Night of the Dark Turtle
programme.desc: When Donatello receives a jolt of electrical energy, he undergoes a radical personality transformation, turning into 'The Dark Turtle', a psychotic caped crimefighter on a mission of revenge against Shredder!

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: As usual, Grizzly picks the door of the Ranger's log cabin to come in and make himself comfortable, but this time it's impossible to enter. The Lemmings have requisitioned the cabin for a party and barricaded themselves inside.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings have got their hands on some white beans which have very strange properties, when mixed with soda anyone who eats them turns into a bouncing ball. Grizzy realises that if he wants to have a nap, he must get rid of the magic beans.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzly wants to get rid of the old piano in the garage at any cost, for the Lemmings love using it to make a dissonant racket. But when he notices a she-bear he's in love with likes the melody of Fur Elise, he decides to learn the piece.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings inherit a crate that fell off a Canadian army convoy transporting ultra-secret material. The case inside the crate emits a sort of laser beam that divides objects into multiple miniaturised versions of themselves.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Near the supermarket, a machine hands out surprise eggs in exchange for a coin. Amongst the lots to be won, there is a super firework rocket the Lemmings dream of.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: At the Ranger's home, Grizzy finds a high-tech gadget that enables you to learn anything within seconds thanks to electrodes hooked to the brain, meaning that he can become the perfect bear.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: An old metal box found by chance in a recess of a cliff brings back childhood memories for Grizzy and the Lemmings.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy struggles to watch a documentary on salmon fish, nice and comfy on the sofa, as the Lemmings play chemistry novices with a fun experiment box.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy's TV remote control is stolen by a partridge who collects high-tech objects in his nest perched high up in a tree. This is a case of absolute urgency.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy has got it into his head to offer a butterfly to the bear he's in love with. But the Lemmings fight with him over the rare specimens he's spotted.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings have discovered an addictive new game based on the astonishing bouncing properties of the ranger's new running shoes.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy babysits for the she-bear he has a crush on. There's no question of Grizzy tripping up, as he has no intention of taking any risks, but he hasn't reckoned on the Lemmings.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Unwilling to share the food both he and the Lemmings are wild about, Grizzy locks it up inside the ranger's brand new and apparently burglar-proof safe.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy is impressed by the TV show of a magician who transforms a partridge into a bouquet of flowers. To get rid of the Lemmings once and for all, for they exasperate him with their incessant games, Grizzy tries the magic trick on them.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy's TV remote control is stolen by a partridge who collects high-tech objects. This is a case of absolute urgency. Grizzy has no other choice but to requisition the Lemmings' propulsion game to recover his possession.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings have discovered a truly wild new game, an augmented reality programme on the ranger's digital tablet. Grizzy confiscates this toy and becomes a fan of the salmon fishing game.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The only edible food item left in the ranger's cabin is a salmon stuck inside a block of ice. Starving, Grizzly decides to melt the ice with the blow-dryer. Only the Lemmings are already using it for their latest rocket game in the piping system.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings have unearthed a box filled with birthday fountains of light that spit forth giant bursts of sparks. They have a blast, but their new game is not really compatible with a bear's nap.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy's last jar of chocolate spread has been pinched by the Lemmings, who are savouring it in secret.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Wedged in his sofa to relieve his aching back, Grizzly baulks at getting up. He designs clever strategies to do everything from a distance, including confiscating the Lemming's radio-controlled toy robot transformer, which he will use as a butler.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The vibrations created by Grizzly's snoring during his nap give the Lemmings an idea for a bouncing game. But when Grizzly changes positions and his snoring stops, the Lemmings must find other sources of vibrations.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy finds a birthday piñata in the ranger's living room. The idea of bursting open a papier-mâché statue with a bat as it says in the instructions pleases him.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Inside a mysterious cave, Grizzy and the Lemmings discover a magical Game of the Goose. The events displayed in the squares that the players land on come true in real life.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy is bothered by an object stuck in between the sofa cushions. It's a big fur lemming puppet glove and the Lemmings believe that the puppet is a Super Lemming who's stronger than Grizzy and they start to obey it.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzly discovers the leftovers of a Halloween party in the ranger's cabin, which was entirely redecorated for the occasion.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: When the last jar of chocolate spread they were fighting over sinks to the bottom of the river, Grizzy and the Lemmings have no choice but to dive in to recover it. But to their great surprise, they discover you can't breathe in water.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy finds a strange figurine with the power to switch the spirits of two living beings and finds himself imprisoned in the body of a caribou.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: While salmon fishing on the riverbank, Grizzly hauls in an unusual catch: an old hand mirror straight out of a fairytale; instead of showing the reflection of the person who holds it, the mirror shows an idealized vision of his surroundings.

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: After arriving on Potpourri Island, our heroes discover the Pokémon Orienteering competition just about to take place and decide to enter, vying for the coveted Pokémon Teamwork medal!

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: In the Wisteria Town Pokémon Contest, May discovers she is to compete against her old rival Harley.

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: As our heroes continue toward May’s next Pokémon Contest, Ash’s Grovyle finds itself nursing a crush on Nurse Joy’s Meganium!

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: After meeting a new Coordinator named Brianna who’s a fan of hers and an even bigger fan of Drew’s, May discovers she must battle her in order to win her third Pokémon Contest ribbon.

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: After a frustrated Sceptile runs off into the forest, Ash and Pikachu go after it and run into a swarm of angry Beedrill—and the next Frontier Brain!

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: It’s time for Ash’s next Battle Frontier challenge: a battle in the impressive outdoor arena at the Battle Palace, against Frontier Brain Spenser!

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: On their way to Ash’s next Battle Frontier challenge, our heroes come across a couple of Pokémon TV stars who aren’t feeling well, so Brock’s Bonsly and James’s Mime Jr. act as stand-ins!

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: After hearing stories about many Trainers and Pokémon being defeated and taunted by a mysterious talking Pokémon known as Red Lightning, our heroes decide to find out what’s really going on.

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: When Brock meets up with a lovely Pokémon breeder who used to be a Coordinator, she talks him into competing against May in the Gardenia Pokémon Contest!

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: On their way to the Battle Tower for Ash’s next challenge, our heroes come across a cute little Smoochum who is fiercely guarded by three Jynx, and Ash has to convince them he’s on their side!

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: On their way to the Battle Tower, our heroes meet an amazing person who seems to be able to communicate with Pokémon by sensing their feelings…and more surprises await!

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Three Teletubbies dance through Teletubbyland. The Teletubbies watch some children learn about the number three. Dipsy climbs a steep hill.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Some voice trumpets rise to wish the Tubbies good morning. The Teletubbies watch some children herding sheep. The Teletubbies go in and out, and up and down, round the corner and along to top until it is time for Tubby Custard.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Tubbies watch two boys having their hair cut. A mirror appears in Teletubbyland. Laa-Laa, Dipsy and Tinky Winky liked looking at their reflection in the mirror.

programme.title: Teletubbies

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Cassie & Steve
programme.desc: Cassie was on a downward spiral until she got a friend request from a rapper named Steve. Two and a half years later, Cassie and Steve are engaged - but they have yet to meet.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Anthony & Marq

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Ramon & Paola
programme.desc: Ramon met Paola on Facebook and established an exclusive online relationship. But after many failed attempts to video chat, is she really who he thinks she is?

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Lauren & Derek
programme.desc: Lauren met Derek online years ago. Derek is caring, supporting Lauren after she had a baby with a former boyfriend. Lauren thinks Derek could be the one, so why has he avoided meeting her?

programme.title: Mission: Impossible: The Bride
programme.desc: Casey poses as a young peasant girl from Europe imported to be the bride of Joe Corvin, the top man at getting underworld millions into Swiss banks.

programme.title: Mission: Impossible: Stone Pillow
programme.desc: Guest star Bradford Dillman plays Edison, a former private detective turned extortionist who is blackmailing a powerful crime chief with a roll of film linking him to murder.

programme.title: Mission: Impossible: Image
programme.desc: Barney assumes the identity of a powerful psychic from a Caribbean island kingdom in order to hypnotize a defecting crime syndicate leader into revealing his super secret list of government officials on the take.

programme.title: Mission: Impossible: Committed
programme.desc: Nora Dawson, a witness to a murder, is committed to an asylum and is being systematically driven out of her mind with drugs when the IMF team is given twenty-four hours to save her life.

programme.title: Mission: Impossible: Bag Woman
programme.desc: Posing as a bag woman for a crooked politician, Casey unknowingly carries an attached case locked to her wrist filled with both money and a bomb set to explode upon opening.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Blazing Saddles
programme.desc: Ton and Allen head to a Hollywood auction and face a new competitor with a seemingly endless cash flow. After they uncover a triple barrel shotgun and an antique camel saddle, Allen confronts an old childhood fear.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Without a Chute
programme.desc: After a tough auction, things get worse when the shop's landlord wants to talk to the guys. Ton tries to negotiate with him while Allen has to put an experimental powered parachute to the test to save the day's profits.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Cashville, Tennessee
programme.desc: Ton and Allen hit the road for Nashville, where they rumble with a band of tough, high-bidding bikers. The day explodes when the guys discover a 37 millimeter anti-tank gun and a cowboy's rodeo saddle.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Carolyn Goes Topless
programme.desc: A friendly competition with former employee Carolyn turns into a vicious battle for a 1968 Corvette Stingray, giving Carolyn the last laugh. Allen tests his Ninja skills with an antique sickle weapon.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Little-Ton Jones
programme.desc: Allen & Ton try to get the inside scoop on their competition by chatting up local auction buyers. The guys find some heavy gold-mining machinery from the 1950s & an amateur rocket that can travel faster than the speed of sound!

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Win Lose or Joust
programme.desc: Ton and Allen race back from an auction to purchase a rare comic valued at over $100,000. The guys uncover a Samurai sword used for ritual suicide and Allen lives out his childhood fantasy of becoming knight.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Gone in 20 Seconds
programme.desc: Ton and Allen get taken down by their fiercest opponent, CJ, but a twist of fate keeps the guys in the game. They uncover a Geiger counter and NASCAR impact wrenches, while their accountant delivers some shocking news.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Animal Instincts
programme.desc: The first pump action shotgun - a Winchester Model 1897; a hunter's collection of prize big game, full-body taxidermy.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Don't Taze Me Bro
programme.desc: Two fierce opponents best Ton and Allen at the auction, leaving the duo in the lurch with a "trash unit" and little chance of breaking even for the day. And Ton gets tazed.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Ton-Geons & Dragons
programme.desc: While Ton soups up their new truck, Allen heads to an auction solo and encounters Carolyn and CJ. There he discovers LARP weaponry, a sword collection, and falconry gear, before Ton reveals their new ride: Betty.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Voodoo Moola
programme.desc: Allen & Ton hit a storage auction in Louisiana's Capitol City and quickly find themselves in trouble with the law! The guys uncover a pair of racing lawnmowers and then stumble on an antique barber chair from the early 1900s!

programme.title: Teen Mom: Standing Up

programme.title: Teen Mom: Baby Steps

programme.title: Teen Mom: Happy Birthday

programme.title: The Maury Show: Is My Sister's Boyfriend My Baby's Dad?

programme.title: The Maury Show: 7 Babies. 8 Men. Who's the Father?

programme.title: The Maury Show: He's My Husband Now. Not Your Baby's Dad!

programme.title: The Maury Show: I Cheated with 97 Women. Will My Fiancé Forgive Me?
programme.desc: Did Rey cheat on Ashley while she was pregnant with his baby and have a secret threesome behind her back? Plus, Jason is worried that his uncle is paying his fiancé to sleep with him. His uncle has a shocking confession that you have to see!

programme.title: The Maury Show: We Had A Threesome…But I'm Not Your Baby's Dad!

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: SECRET CULTS AND ORDERS
programme.desc: Top 10 Secrets and Mysteries looks at secret cults and mysterious orders including Skull and Bones at Yale University, the Freemasons, Thule and Vril Societies, the Bullingdon Club, the Hermetic Order of the Golden Dawn

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: SCIENCE AND MYSTERIES FOR 21. CENTURY
programme.desc: Top 10 Secrets and Mysteries looks at some of the mysteries of modern science including memory of water, reincarnation, the beginning of life, hypnosis, the secrets of Morphic fields, time travel, why we must sleep.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: PEOPLE WITHOUT IDENTITY
programme.desc: 10 Mysterious Figures: Individuals who emerged from obscurity, made their mark on history, then vanished without a trace. Their identities remain unconfirmed, shrouding them in mystery.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: NATURE’S MAGIC
programme.desc: Natural sites worldwide hold spiritual significance for civilizations, from ancient rituals to modern reverence, each crackling with unique energy and a hint of magic.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: MYSTERIOUS WORKS OF ART
programme.desc: Great art and its creators unveil mysteries more intricate than TV dramas. Amidst theft and intrigue, we persist in solving them. Yet, the truth often lies buried with those who created it, adding layers of intrigue to the art world.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: SECRETS AND MAGIC OF ALTERNATIVE MEDICINE
programme.desc: In this episode, we dive into untraditional healing and its mysteries, challenging conventional Western medicine. Explore alternative medical miracles that will captivate you.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: MYSTERIOUS AND UNSOLVED DISSAPEARANCES
programme.desc: Legally declared dead, bodies never found. Disappearances spark speculation, media attention, and unanswered questions. Where did they go?

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: SECRET TECHNOLOGIES
programme.desc: Scientists, engineers, military, and intelligence agencies push boundaries with unlimited budgets and secrecy. This episode unveils known (or suspected) top-secret projects.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In January 2002, fashion journalist Christa Worthington is found murdered in her Truro home. Christopher McCowen is convicted in 2006 of her murder, robbery, and rape, despite his claims of innocence and other DNA found at the scene.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 2001, British traveler Peter Falconio is presumed murdered in the Australian outback. Joanne Lees survives the attack, and Bradley John Murdoch is convicted of the murder in 2005. Peter's body is never found.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1999, NFL player Rae Carruth is convicted for orchestrating the shooting of his pregnant girlfriend. He is sentenced to 18-24 years. Their son, Chancellor, has cerebral palsy.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In February 1977, Australian heiress Janie Shepherd vanishes in London. David Lashley, a convicted rapist, later confesses to her murder in prison. In 1990, he is convicted based on this confession and sentenced to life.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1998, Peter Bergna's car crashes off a mountain, killing his wife Rinette. After suspicions arise and a first trial ends in a hung jury, Bergna is convicted of murder in 2002 following new testimony about his abusive behavior.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1975, 15-year-old Martha Moxley is murdered in Belle Haven, Connecticut. The case goes cold until 2002, when Michael Skakel, a family member, is convicted based on new evidence. Skakel maintains his innocence.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 2007, Meredith Kercher is murdered in Italy. Amanda Knox and Raffaele Sollecito are initially convicted but later exonerated. Rudy Guede is convicted. The Kercher family still seeks justice.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: On December 7, 1993, Colin Ferguson killed six and injured nineteen in the Long Island Railroad Massacre. Defending himself at trial, he bizarrely questioned his own victims and was sentenced to over 300 years in prison.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1995 Paris, Guy Georges, a serial killer, was linked to multiple murders through DNA and later confessed in court. He was sentenced to life in prison, leading to the establishment of a DNA database for offenders in France.

programme.title: Naked Science: Making Mountains
programme.desc: The Rockies. Magnificent and awe-inspiring mysterious, the Rockies are one of the longest mountain ranges in the world. Naked Science investigates the geological processes that build these massive mountains.

programme.title: Naked Science: Anatomy Of Tornado
programme.desc: Tornados are one of the most destructive forces of the planets with winds at 300 miles an hour. They can destroy buildings and hurl cars hundreds of feet through the air.

programme.title: Naked Science: Atmosphere
programme.desc: We breathe it every day, it protects us from solar radiation, controls our weather – it’s the atmosphere. Naked Science takes a trip from the earth to near space to understand how the earth’s atmosphere affects all life on the planet.

programme.title: Naked Science: The Big Bang - The First Three Seconds
programme.desc: 13.7 billion years ago there was absolutely nothing. No space, no time, and no matter. Naked Science follows the first three seconds of creation when a speck of light appeared from nothing and rapidly expanded into an enormous Universe.

programme.title: Naked Science: Magnetic Earth
programme.desc: The Earth hurtles through space protected by an invisible force field, known as a magnetosphere.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Flight Status
programme.desc: The latest updates on the new space hardware about to launch us into the next generation of space flight some rockets and capsules are on time others not so fortunate.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Invasion on Mars
programme.desc: The EXO Mars and Curiosity continue their scientific discoveries as we prepare new missions both robotic and manned to the Red planet Mars.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Juno, Cassini and Beyond
programme.desc: The Cassini space craft has completed its mission over Saturn, Juno has reached its target and has commenced its scientific observations of the giant planet Jupiter.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Slingshot
programme.desc: Sending probes throughout the solar system is a mathematicians dream and an engineers nightmare, sling shotting probes around other planets to arrive at their destination.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Living Planet
programme.desc: With continuous observation of arth from orbit scientists have built a data set over time that reveals much about how the Earth breaths and life sustains itself on this living Planet.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Cosmic Collisions
programme.desc: Cosmic and x rays, these mysterious and highly powerful particles from deep space are being investigated to finally discover where they come from.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Gravity
programme.desc: LISA pathfinder, has led the way for a space based gravity wave detector, what are gravity waves and why are they so hard to detect?

programme.title: The New Frontier: Interface
programme.desc: The Ionisphere, where the Earth interfaces with space, is little understood, it effects radio communications, GPS signals and satellites alike.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Mercury Or Bust
programme.desc: The ESA - JAXA joint Bepi-Colombo Mission is moments away from launch.

programme.title: Jungle Planet

programme.title: Wildest Arctic: The Tundra: Desert of Ice

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Starchild
programme.desc: The Turtles face a crisis of galactic proportions when they must play babysitter to an alien infant with incredible powers.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Legend of Koji

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Convicts from Dimension X

programme.desc: The Turtles must once again help their Turtleoid friend Kurma defend the planet Shellri-La from alien invaders.

programme.desc: The Turtles join forces with Merdude, a strange fish-creature, and journey with him to the fabulous underwater city of Atlantis.

programme.title: Paranormal State: The Ladder
programme.desc: A large family in Shrewsbury, MA, has been living with a spirit for the last 40 years. To most of the siblings, the entity is menacing and evil, but Sandy seems to have a different connection.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Pet Cemetery
programme.desc: Ryan and his team investigate the bizarre haunting of a woman and her dog in Waterboro, Maine. The Labrador was chewing at her own fur and standing on guard on the bed, growling at unseen figures.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Bedroom Eyes
programme.desc: PRS travels to rural Pennsylvania to investigate a haunting that began after a new couple moved into an old family farm.?Over the next three days, hidden tragedies are uncovered and latent psychic abilities are revealed.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Family Ties
programme.desc: Jimmie is haunted by the apparition of a mysterious old woman who peers at him in the bathroom of his family's Petersburg, VA home.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Vegas
programme.desc: The PRS travels to Sin City to investigate a 14-year old medium who claims to speak to a dead girl.

programme.title: Paranormal State: The Messenger
programme.desc: Kelli and her husband Riley are frightened in their coastal Oregon home. They are plagued by a headless apparition and often hear mysterious noises that resemble cries for help from the dark corners of their house.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Suicide Possession
programme.desc: Joan and her boyfriend Tony are seeing apparitions in their New Jersey home. Both are concerned that the spirits are influencing their behavior: a non-drinker, Tony has turned to the bottle while Joan has thoughts of harming herself.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Halsham House
programme.desc: Nestled into the British countryside is Halsham house where a poltergeist hides in wait for the team with frightening effect. With bones found in the attic have the team pushed it too far.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Mansion House
programme.desc: Yvette takes her fearless team to a onetime house, onetime museum and now a care home to investigate the strange goings on reported by staff and guests alike. It doesn’t take long for Yvette to regret her decision to go it alone in the cold dark cellar.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Walton Hall
programme.desc: The Most Haunted team visit a gem in the Cheshire countryside where a disembodied voice brings disarray to the investigation and something in the cellar fills Yvette with fear and dread.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Oak House

programme.title: Most Haunted: Middleton Hall
programme.desc: Yvette and her team are called to a grade II listed building that spans over 900 years in an attempt to uncover the mysterious paranormal activity that is said to occur there.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Jen & Skylar
programme.desc: After being bullied in her past, Jen relies on online communities to establish relationships. After meeting Skylar online, they immediately hit it off. But they've never met in person!

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Artis & Jess
programme.desc: Five months ago Artis met Jess online. Artis and Jess each were in relationships, but believed they'd found true love. But is Jess everything that she says she is? (S2, ep9)

programme.desc: For over seven years, Ashley has sent dramatically altered photos of herself to her soul mate, Mike, to make her look thinner and more attractive to him. (S2, ep10)

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Aaliyah & Alicia
programme.desc: Aaliyah has been able to share her life struggles with Alicia. However, despite living only 70 blocks away, Alicia refuses to meet her as she hasn't come out to her friends and family yet. (S2, ep11)

programme.title: Mission: Impossible: Bag Woman
programme.desc: Posing as a bag woman for a crooked politician, Casey unknowingly carries an attached case locked to her wrist filled with both money and a bomb set to explode upon opening.

programme.title: Mission: Impossible: Double Dead
programme.desc: Gangsters running a loan-shark racket in Hawaii short-circuit the IMF team's investigation when they capture Willy and feed him gigantic doses of truth serum.

programme.title: Mission: Impossible: Casino

programme.title: Mission: Impossible: Trapped

programme.title: Mission: Impossible: Break!

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Voodoo Moola
programme.desc: Allen & Ton hit a storage auction in Louisiana's Capitol City and quickly find themselves in trouble with the law! The guys uncover a pair of racing lawnmowers and then stumble on an antique barber chair from the early 1900s!

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Dead Aim
programme.desc: Ton & Allen face-off against an auction buyer with shady bidding tactics and even more questionable fashion sense. The guys find a custom-built monowheel & a rifle designed to ward off grave robbers in the early 19th century!

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Choo-Choo, Cha-Ching!
programme.desc: Ton and Allen get a surprise second chance after nearly getting shut out in the small town of Palmer. They uncover a motorized railroad maintenance car from the 1960's and a pre-WWI Japanese semi-automatic pistol.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Diesel Digs
programme.desc: The guys head to the industrial hub of LA to score some heavy machinery and unearth a 1981 Bobcat 743 Frontloader, one of the first powered by a diesel engine and a trench-defending WWI German Sturmtrupp helmet bashing mace.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Big Sis Ducks Out
programme.desc: Big Sis drops some bad news as the guys clash with Carolyn over a carnival unit at an auction in Long Beach. Ton and Allen unearth a Native American tomahawk, a dunk tank and a rare gun that can fire three shots at once.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Home on the Gun Range
programme.desc: Barely making it to an auction in Corona, CA, Allen and Ton buy an overstuffed unit and find an antique crossbow, a rare 10-gauge Winchester shotgun and a 1940s Kiss-o-Meter.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Gold 'n' Gloves
programme.desc: The Auction Hunters team goes toe-to-toe with Ton's evil twin! They find a medieval catapult designed for storming castles and Ton uncovers a hidden collection of gold bars worth thousands of dollars!

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Strat'ed for Cash
programme.desc: On a limited budget in the historic L.A. neighborhood of West Adams, Allen and Ton still win three lockers and find a Maui Blue Fender Stratocaster and a 1930s Schwinn bike.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Gangs of New York
programme.desc: Ton and Allen duke it out with a local thrift store gang at a Brooklyn storage auction. The guys discover a classic arcade redemption game and a mid-century water cannon designed to fight towering infernos!

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Pucks for Bucks
programme.desc: The guys get stuck with a unit they didn't want and have to sort through trash to make their bottom line. They uncover glass blowing tools, autographed baseballs and a hockey puck launcher, which puts Ton in the line of fire.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Nothing But Net
programme.desc: Ton and Allen face their toughest challenge yet when two old rivals join forces. The guys dig up an off-road motorcycle and a pair of net guns, while the staff clashes with mystery shoppers back at Haff Ton Pawn.

programme.title: Teen Mom: Happy Birthday

programme.title: Arthur: Sue Ellen Gets Her Goose Cooked/Best of The Nest

programme.title: Arthur: Francine's Pilfered Paper/Buster Gets Real
programme.desc: When Francine has a big paper over Thanksgiving, she copies from a website. There's nothing wrong with that, is there? Everyone loves the new Top Supermarket reality series on TV, except Arthur. Will it end his friendship with Buster?

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Let's Go For A Ride!
programme.desc: Barney and the children have fun playing with and learning about busses and cars.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: The Good Egg
programme.desc: When the kids find a mysterious, colorful egg in the park, Barney takes them on a trip to Kenya to try to find out what kind of animal could have laid the egg.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Big Brother Rusty
programme.desc: Barney's excited to find out that Rusty's family is adopting a new baby girl from China; however, Rusty feels differently. He's not sure if he's going to be a good brother, and he doesn't know much about China.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: BJ learns that having a pet is a bigger responsibility than he expected when he takes care of a friend’s dog. When Baby Bop’s favorite teddy bear is torn, she wants to take him to the vet for help.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Butterflies - Nature Of Things
programme.desc: Baby Bop is sad because she lost her pet caterpillar, Monty. Barney and the others try to help her find it. Baby Bop is surprised to discover Monty inside a chrysalis. Everyone is excited to find Monty—now a beautiful butterfly!

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Once Upon a Fairy Tale
programme.desc: Mario is trying to write his very own fairy tale. The children help by acting out some of their favorite fairy tales to give Mario some ideas.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Playing Games - No, No, No!
programme.desc: Baby Bop has trouble following the rules and taking turns while playing games. Barney and Riff help her learn how to follow the rules. In the end, Baby Bop and BJ have fun playing together, and Baby Bop learns that rules make games fun for everyone!

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Gift Of The Dinos - Winter
programme.desc: It's Christmas and BJ dreams of getting a new baseball glove to go with his baseball, while Riff hopes for new drumsticks to use his drum. Barney reminds them that Christmas is more than just getting gifts and helps them learn the meaning of Christmas.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: The Park Kids decide to have a Dog Show to show off their pets. A rainy day has everyone stuck in the Caboose. Barney suggests fun activities.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Atlantis Awakes
programme.desc: The Turtles join forces with Merdude, a strange fish-creature, and journey with him to the fabulous underwater city of Atlantis.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Dirk Savage: Mutant Hunter
programme.desc: When eccentric zillionaire A.J. Howard hires Dirk Savage, a professional mercenary, to rid the city of its 'mutant problem', the first mutants he goes after are the Turtles!

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Invasion of the Krangazoids
programme.desc: It's trouble for the Turtles when Krang makes five clones of himself and sends them up to do his bidding in the city.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Combat Land
programme.desc: The Turtles are invited to be the first guests at combat Land, an amusement park where visitors fight a wide variety of robotic warriors from various historical eras.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Shredder Triumphant!
programme.desc: The Turtles face their greatest challenge ever when they become trapped in Dimension X--while Shredder and Krang bring the Technodrome out of the ocean depths and at last conquer the city!

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Get Shredder!
programme.desc: Shredder & Krang are still loose on Earth, but without the resources of the Technodrome, they plan to steal equipment from the abandoned Hall of Science at the World Fair.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Wrath of the Rat King
programme.desc: Burne has created a hot line for callers to use should they spot those responsible for the destruction of the Channel Six News Building. Inside the Hall of Science, Shredder is fearful that the Turtles will eventually find a way into the building.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Cyber-Turtles
programme.desc: While investigating the theft of a rare telescope, the Astro-Viewer, the Turtles discover odd tracks leading away from the National Space Exploration Centre.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): State of Shock
programme.desc: After defeating a mysterious group of ninjas at the Emeryville Power Station, the Turtles decode part of a map that they were able to liberate from the would-be thieves.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Cry H.A.V.O.C.!
programme.desc: The Turtles get the drop on masked thieves hijacking a jewellery store, only to have themselves arrested for the crime! Before the turtles can be booked, they are rescued by the mutants Amok and Overdrive.

programme.title: Total Drama Island
programme.desc: The campers take part in three trust challenges.

programme.title: Total Drama Island
programme.desc: The campers are sent to boot camp; the only rule is going until everyone drops out, and one camper is left standing.

programme.title: Total Drama Island
programme.desc: The campers are challenged to participate in some extreme sports.

programme.title: Total Drama Island
programme.desc: The campers must eat disgusting stuff.

programme.title: Total Drama Island
programme.desc: The teams are officially dissolved. It's every ruthless camper for themselves in a high stakes game of no pain, no game.

programme.title: Total Drama Island
programme.desc: The teams search for keys to treasure chests.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy has got it into his head to offer a butterfly to the bear he's in love with. But the Lemmings fight with him over the rare specimens he's spotted.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings have discovered an addictive new game based on the astonishing bouncing properties of the ranger's new running shoes.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy babysits for the she-bear he has a crush on. There's no question of Grizzy tripping up, as he has no intention of taking any risks, but he hasn't reckoned on the Lemmings.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Unwilling to share the food both he and the Lemmings are wild about, Grizzy locks it up inside the ranger's brand new and apparently burglar-proof safe.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy is impressed by the TV show of a magician who transforms a partridge into a bouquet of flowers. To get rid of the Lemmings once and for all, for they exasperate him with their incessant games, Grizzy tries the magic trick on them.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy's TV remote control is stolen by a partridge who collects high-tech objects. This is a case of absolute urgency. Grizzy has no other choice but to requisition the Lemmings' propulsion game to recover his possession.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings have discovered a truly wild new game, an augmented reality programme on the ranger's digital tablet. Grizzy confiscates this toy and becomes a fan of the salmon fishing game.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The only edible food item left in the ranger's cabin is a salmon stuck inside a block of ice. Starving, Grizzly decides to melt the ice with the blow-dryer. Only the Lemmings are already using it for their latest rocket game in the piping system.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings have unearthed a box filled with birthday fountains of light that spit forth giant bursts of sparks. They have a blast, but their new game is not really compatible with a bear's nap.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy's last jar of chocolate spread has been pinched by the Lemmings, who are savouring it in secret.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Wedged in his sofa to relieve his aching back, Grizzly baulks at getting up. He designs clever strategies to do everything from a distance, including confiscating the Lemming's radio-controlled toy robot transformer, which he will use as a butler.

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: On their way to the Grand Festival, our heroes help a berry farmer reclaim his stolen crop.

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: As the Grand Festival gets under way at the Indigo Plateau, May has her hands full trying to compete with the best Coordinators in the world, including Harley, Drew, and new friend Solidad!

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: The Grand Festival is going strong as May faces down one impressive competitor after another!

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: May faces Drew in the quarterfinals of the Grand Festival, and the two seem to be very evenly matched! Can May come out ahead?

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: On their way to Ash’s rematch with Brandon at the Battle Pyramid in Fennel Valley, our heroes come across a village taking part in the annual ritual of choosing a king, and Ash’s Aipom becomes a most unwilling candidate!

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: Our heroes meet an aspiring comedian named Audrey and her showbiz sidekick and Pokémon partner, Chatot.

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: Our heroes find themselves embroiled in Pokémon politics as two Weavile face off for leadership of a group of Sneasel!

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: When our heroes arrive at the Battle Pyramid for Ash’s battle with Brandon, they discover a Nurse Joy who’s a big fan of Ash and May!

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: After their recent defeats in important matches at the Battle Pyramid and the Grand Festival, Ash and May are feeling a little down, until several old friends come together to support them.

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: When Nurse Joy becomes too ill to take care of the Pokémon Center, our heroes pitch in, coming to the aid of a Pokésitter and his charges after a bus accident.

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle
programme.desc: Ash prepares for his final rematch with Brandon at the Battle Pyramid by rounding up the Pokémon he’s been with the longest, spending his time sharing old memories with them and training intensely.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A voice trumpet rises and Laa-Laa and Dipsy play a going up and going down game. They go up the hill and then they go down the hill. They do it again and again.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A voice trumpet rises and teaches the Teletubbies a nursery rhyme. Then they watch Becky go for a ride on a Tram. Po rides her scooter too fast and knocks the other Tubbies over.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Dipsy says "Eh-oh" and runs off to find the other Tubbies. They watch some children make fantastic animals from papermache.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Laa-Laa fells like giving all the other Teletubbies a big hug. But where's Tinky Winky? The Teletubbies watch some children who are going for a walk in the woods.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: It's time for toast in Teletubbyland. The Teletubbies and a group of children see Tamzin Griffin pretend to be Funny Lady and tell the story of the Yellow Cow.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies bounce up and down until they spill their Tubby Custard. The Teletubbies watch a boy digging for potatoes with his Grandmother.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies dance around the hills until it is time to watch some children making mosaics from different shapes. Tinky Winky and Po play an in and out game.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky sees Dipsy on top of the House. The Teletubbies watch some children playing a on top and underneath game.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Today, the Teletubbies are relaxing, until it is time to watch Andy Brown and some children look in a stream to see what they can find. The Teletubbies are trying to be quiet, but Po is making too much noise.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A magic watering can appears in Teletubbyland, and Po has fun watering everything and watching them grow. The Teletubbies watch two children make ice cream sundaes with jelly, ice cream and other nice foods.

programme.desc: When a government experiment goes awry it renders an innocent bystander invisible.

programme.title: The Negotiator

programme.title: Street Kings

programme.title: Alien Strain

programme.desc: The hunt for Cursed Treasure begins in Cheshire, England where a discovery by peat-cutters revealed a story of violence, ritual sacrifice and even whispers of a curse.

programme.title: Deadly Women

programme.title: Deadly Women

programme.title: Psychic Witness

programme.title: Psychic Witness

programme.title: Psychic Witness
programme.desc: In Louisiana, a young man meets a mystery woman, then vanishes. In New Jersey, a teenage girl disappears walking home from school. In each case, a psychic saw everything.

programme.title: Psychic Witness

programme.title: Cold Blood: Kill Your Parents

programme.desc: Hogan's operation is known to a Gestapo man who wants to trade his knowledge for $1,000,000 in diamonds, but can he be trusted?

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Cry H.A.V.O.C.!
programme.desc: The Turtles get the drop on masked thieves hijacking a jewellery store, only to have themselves arrested for the crime! Before the turtles can be booked, they are rescued by the mutants Amok and Overdrive.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): H.A.V.O.C. in the Streets!
programme.desc: Titanus sends of couple of his H.A.V.O.C.mutants to bust into the aerospace plant and steal some much needed equipment. Only problem is that the Turtles were waiting for them.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Enter: Krakus
programme.desc: Krakus, a cop from the future, arrives and thinking that the Turtles are in league with Titanus, shakes them down for information.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Turtle Trek
programme.desc: Krang activates his new dimensional portal, thanks to Shredder's installation of the Energy Transmitter at the Power Relay Station, giving him access to Dimension X.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Unknown Ninja
programme.desc: While watching video tapes of old battles, Raphael sees a mystery person spying on them. While the Turtles investigate, an alien warlord named Dregg sends his TechnoGansters to steal all the gold on Earth.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Dregg of the Earth
programme.desc: April contacts the Turtles to let them know that Dregg has landed on Earth and is preparing a press conference. The Turtles rush to meet April at the Park, where they hear Dregg announce his intentions and offers the world his molecular converter.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Wrath of Medusa
programme.desc: Believing that the Turtles are the only thing that stands between him and total domination of the planet, Dregg makes contact with Medusa, a sinisterly beautiful alien.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Showdown
programme.desc: April contacts the Turtles to inform them that all the TV stations in the City are jammed. Sleazebug tries to recruit the Turtles for a martial arts tournament.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Split-Second
programme.desc: Carter, April and the Turtles investigate a break-in at the Antique Timepiece Museum. Convinced that Dregg is responsible, the Turtles discover a clue on a wall display.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Carter the Enforcer
programme.desc: Splinter convinces the Turtles that it is their duty to fight evil, even when the whole world is against them. Carter and the Turtles team up with April to get the goods on Dregg.
